scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a cultural-program-specific handoff package with explicit owner transfer, booking and roster artifacts, accessibility context, and blocker state.
Missing house count, accessible-seat status, will-call, room assignment, and destination-acceptance prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership and conflicting calendar or staffing records were escalated with an owner map instead of dropped into generic event coordination.
Booking, deposit, roster, and promotion conflicts were preserved as an evidence-backed mismatch and routed without unsupported refund or pricing adjudication.
Refund, fee, and seating-exception requests outside documented authority were rejected and handed off with the right records.
Guest-safety and suspicious-ticketing conditions were escalated to the correct owners rather than over-resolved in-lane.
The lane refused to absorb booking updates, reseating, calendar edits, and complaint-resolution execution work and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
High-volume festival and timed-entry handling preserved queue control and did not bypass prerequisite or policy checks.
Missing tenant authority, screening, accessibility, and source-of-truth maps correctly triggered a low-confidence blocked-dependency escalation.
Conflicting public and tenant customer-impact rules were escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive instruction.
Stricter tenant membership, screening, and group-arrival controls were applied without breaking the public baseline or inventing new lane authority.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required booking, roster, accessibility, timing, and service-ticket artifacts were explicit.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to cultural-program coordination, booking and roster dependencies, owner routing, and guest-impact boundaries.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the issue contract.
